prioritizedEntities.name: Anthropic
prioritizedEntities.volatilityNote: Composite 60.0 sits at exact Functional/Established band boundary (60/61). Any score change risks band crossing in either direction.
prioritizedEntities.trigger: HOLD EXPIRES TODAY. Multi-vector assessment trigger: (1) RSP v3 published Feb 26 — dropped pre-deployment safety guarantee pledge under Pentagon pressure (CNN, Time, Engadget: 'Anthropic Drops Flagship Safety Pledge'); (2) Maintained two hard red lines: autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance; (3) March 26 court injunction (Judge Lin: 'Orwellian') restores federal agency Claude access — Anthropic won preliminary injunction; (4) Claude Opus 4.7 released; (5) Mythos Preview (April 7) — cybersecurity dual-use to select partner orgs; (6) White House carve-out drafting active (CNBC April 10: Vance/Bessent consulted pre-Mythos release); (7) Moonshot R&D target shifted May 5 (May 15 → September 30) — signals internal resource allocation change.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: -3.0 to -8.0 (RSP pledge drop is major negative across SYS/INT dimensions) offset by +2.0 to +4.0 (court win, red lines maintained, Transparency Hub, Opus 4.7 safety evaluations). Net: likely -1.0 to -5.0. Band-crossing risk is significant at 60.0.
prioritizedEntities.notes: This is the most structurally complex hold-release in the current queue. The RSP pledge drop (Feb 26) has been widely covered and represents a measurable SYS regression — Anthropic's core organizational identity was built on this commitment. However, the maintained red lines (autonomous weapons, domestic surveillance) and the successful court fight against Pentagon blacklisting are genuine positive ACC/INT signals that partially offset. The Mythos Preview dual-use deployment requires careful dimensional analysis: is limited-partner cybersecurity deployment consistent with prior safety commitments? The court injunction win and 'Orwellian' framing from the judge represents a significant external validation of Anthropic's safety position. Assess with all five evidence vectors in scope. Do not rely solely on the negative RSP coverage — the dual-positive/negative evidence structure requires full dimensional analysis.

Altman — Microsoft potentially liable for 'aiding/abetting breach of charitable trust' (jury question #3); Nadella testified May 11, defended $100B+ investment; (2) Mass layoffs — 8,750 US employees voluntary buyout (Rule of 70, first in 51yr history, May 7 notifications, 30-day window); LinkedIn 1,000 cuts (5%); (3) Suleyman statement: 'most professional tasks automated in 12-18 months' — AI-displacement-as-policy framing from Microsoft AI division head.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: Without verdict: layoff dimension -1.0 to -2.0 (EMP: AI-driven labor displacement framing from senior leadership). With adverse verdict on aiding/abetting: additional -3.0 to -6.0 (ACC/SYS). Verdict pending — assess now, note verdict as forward signal.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Assess the two confirmed evidence streams (trial testimony + layoffs) now. Flag the verdict as a forward-pending item in the assessment output. Suleyman's 'all professional tasks automated' prediction is not compassion-relevant as a standalone statement, but in context of simultaneous large-scale layoffs it becomes an EMP-dimension signal: leadership explicitly frames the layoffs as part of an AI-displacement program, not a temporary restructuring. The 'Rule of 70' buyout structure (age + years of service = 70+) disproportionately affects older workers — this is an EQU dimension signal. Document with forward note: verdict expected week of May 18-21; if adverse finding on aiding/abetting, re-assess post-verdict.

prioritizedEntities.name: India
prioritizedEntities.volatilityNote: Composite 32.4. Boundary at 40 (Developing/Functional upper). Boundary at 20 (Critical lower). At 32.4, downward movement of -12 could trigger Critical crossing — significant risk given compound negative evidence.
prioritizedEntities.trigger: NEW CRITICAL EVENT (May 8, 2026): Indian Navy personnel forced 38-40 Rohingya refugees — including minors as young as 15, elderly up to 66, and critically ill individuals (cancer, TB, heart disease, diabetes) — off a naval vessel into the Andaman Sea with life jackets only, making them swim to Myanmar. All were UNHCR-registered refugees detained under false pretenses (biometric data collection) May 6, flown to Port Blair, transferred to naval vessel May 8. UN Special Rapporteur: 'unconscionable, unacceptable acts.' India has not officially confirmed or commented. This is a new major PRO/ACC event DISTINCT from May 2025 incident referenced in May 14 scan.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: -3.0 to -6.0 (Navy Rohingya sea-deportation is a new floor-adjacent conduct event on top of existing evidence cluster). Note: May 14 applied score (30.5) is the working baseline — do NOT re-propose 34.4 → 30.5.
prioritizedEntities.notes: The May 8 Rohingya sea-deportation is material new evidence dated after the May 6 assessment. This is not the same as the May 2025 incident documented in prior scans. May 2026 incident involves: UNHCR-registered refugees, naval vessel, forced into open sea (not deportation to a country, but into open water), explicit deception ('biometric data collection'), vulnerable populations (minors, elderly, terminally ill). This is a PRO-dimension floor-conduct event (protection of refugees — UNHCR registered) AND an ACC-dimension event (government non-accountability, no official statement). Combined with: Transgender Amendment Bill (March 2026), May 9 Nagpur Instagram arrest (Operation Sindoor), online censorship expansion rules (April 2026) — this is a four-vector compound negative. Working baseline is 30.5 (applied May 14), not 32.4 (rotation-state not yet updated). Assess from 30.5 with new Rohingya event as primary trigger.

Euronews: 'Russia's attack laid bare all the hypocrisy with which it negotiated the fragile truce' (Macron). 652 drones and 41 missiles intercepted — Russia launched what appears to be the largest single-day combined drone/missile assault of the post-ceasefire period.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: floor — no score change from 0. Document: post-ceasefire offensive surge, civilian infrastructure targeting, 24 killed/many injured May 14-15 attack, hypocrisy of ceasefire-concurrent negotiations.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Floor entity. Last assessed: never (null in rotation-state). This is a first baseline combined with the most acute single-day event since ceasefire expiry. Document three evidence categories: (1) May 14-15 mass attack (24 killed, residential building collapse, multi-city strikes); (2) Post-ceasefire conduct pattern (1,560+ drones/56 missiles single day, 150+ engagements during declared ceasefire May 9-11); (3) Ceasefire negotiation hypocrisy — attacks intensified during active ceasefire talks. Macron quote is useful INT-dimension framing from a major state actor. No score change from 0 — document as ongoing floor conduct with new conduct-category evidence.

The trial proceedings (child exploitation, grooming, addiction) are pending, adding a third track. Assess this cycle alongside xAI — both AI labs with documented children's safety failures and active multi-jurisdictional regulatory response. Meta's AI age-profiling deployment is a responsive-action signal: classify as ACC-positive only if the response is proportionate and effective, not merely reactive to regulatory pressure.

Indian Navy forces 38-40 UNHCR-registered Rohingya refugees — including minors (15-16 yrs), elderly (up to 66 yrs), and critically ill individuals (cancer, TB, heart disease, diabetes) — into the Andaman Sea from a naval vessel on May 8, 2026.
